content:
- Read the Ralph progress log and both task indexes, then enumerated every active candidate task from `.ralph/current_tasks.md`.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks; the only explicit active blocker is story 22a task 02 blocked by story 22a task 01. All currently selectable work is unblocked failing non-bug work.
- Inspected the actual repo architecture: Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`) plus vendored Go `cockroachdb_molt`, single root `Dockerfile`, single-image README/operator flow, and image-publish workflow. This means the runtime/image split is not implemented yet.
- Compared active task scopes. Story 16 tasks 04/05 are README/operator-flow cleanup tasks that assume published-image-only operation exists, but the codebase still exposes a mixed single-image/local-build architecture. The most logical next foundational task is story 18 task 01 to prune the verify image down to a verify-only source slice before later split-image docs/publish work.
- Next step if context ends immediately: continue from `.ralph/current_task.txt` with story 18 task 01 using TDD, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all pass.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with the selected next task: story 18 task 01 (`verify-http-image` prune-to-verify-only-source-slice).
- Selection followed precedence rules: zero active failing bugs, several active failing non-bug tasks, no higher-priority tags among them, and this task is the strongest architectural unblocker for the remaining split-image stories.
- Next step after this chooser turn: open that task file, use the `tdd` skill, implement the minimal verify-only build slice, and prove `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` still pass.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, the active story-18 task, and the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the task file has no existing `<plan>` and no execution marker yet, so this turn must stay in planning mode until a plan is written.
- Inspected current repo architecture: top-level Dockerfile and CI are still runner-only Rust contracts; the vendored Go module under `cockroachdb_molt/molt` is not part of current `make check`/`make test` lanes.
- Computed the repo-local Go dependency slice for `go list -deps ./cmd/verify`: `cmd/internal/cmdutil`, `cmd/verify`, `comparectx`, `dbconn`, `dbtable`, `moltlogger`, `molttelemetry`, `mysqlconv`, `mysqlurl`, `oracleconv`, `parsectx`, `pgconv`, `retry`, `rowiterator`, `utils`, `utils/typeconv`, `verify`, `verify/dbverify`, `verify/inconsistency`, `verify/rowverify`, `verify/tableverify`, and `verify/verifymetrics`.
- Identified obvious out-of-slice top-level directories for deletion from the verify build path: `.github`, `build`, `climanifest`, `compression`, `demo`, `docker`, `docs`, `e2e`, `fetch`, `moltcsv`, `scaletesting`, `scripts`, and `testutils`, plus root command files that currently still wire `fetch` and extra utility commands into `molt`.
- Read the adjacent story-16 verify-removal plan and story-18 tasks 02/03 to keep boundaries sharp: task 01 should prune source and add proof-of-removal tests, task 02 owns the Go 1.26/dependency refresh, and task 03 owns the scratch verify image packaging.
- Next step: write the story-18 task-01 plan file, add its `<plan>` path to the task file, set the plan terminator to `NOW EXECUTE`, then stop immediately per Ralph instructions.

- Created the story-18 task-01 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/01-task-prune-the-codebase-down-to-a-verify-only-source-slice-and-prove-removal_plans/2026-04-19-verify-source-slice-prune-plan.md` and linked it from the task file via `<plan>...</plan>`.
- Marked the plan terminator as `NOW EXECUTE` because the source-slice boundary is concrete enough: in-place prune of `cockroachdb_molt/molt`, verify-only root command, Rust repo-contract enforcement, `go.mod`/`go.sum` reduction, and explicit `testutils` audit.
- Key design decision for execution: do not make cargo tests depend on `go`, because repo CI currently installs Rust only. Use Rust repo-contract tests to guard the source slice, and use Go commands only as supplemental local sanity checks.
- Important file-level cleanup already identified for execution: delete `cockroachdb_molt/molt/cmd/fetch`, `cockroachdb_molt/molt/fetch`, out-of-slice top-level vendored dirs, `cockroachdb_molt/molt/cmd/escape_password.go`, and `cockroachdb_molt/molt/cmd/internal/cmdutil/pprof.go`; then reduce `go.mod`/`go.sum` and audit whether `testutils` is the only justified test-only exception.
- Next step after quitting immediately due to context limit: read the plan, follow the `tdd` skill strictly, start with a failing Rust repo-contract tracer bullet in `crates/runner/tests/ci_contract.rs` plus new `crates/runner/tests/support/verify_source_contract.rs`, and execute the prune slice by slice until `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass.

- Read startup progress, current task pointer, the story-18 task file, and the linked execution plan.
- Confirmed this turn is execution mode (`NOW EXECUTE`) and gathered current boundaries: `crates/runner/tests/ci_contract.rs`, Rust support modules, and Go root wiring in `cockroachdb_molt/molt/cmd/root.go`.
- Verified the current Go root still imports and exposes `fetch` plus `escape-password`, and the vendored tree still contains many out-of-slice dirs (`docs`, `docker`, `demo`, `e2e`, `fetch`, etc.).
- Next step: add the first failing Rust repo-contract test plus a shared verify-source support module, run that focused test RED, then prune the first obvious verify-out-of-slice entries until it turns GREEN.

- Executed TDD slice 1 and slice 2 for story-18 task-01.
- Added `crates/runner/tests/support/verify_source_contract.rs` and new `ci_contract` coverage for the verify-only vendored source slice.
- RED/GREEN slice 1: contract initially failed on top-level vendored clutter; removed `.githooks`, `.github`, `.gitignore`, `Makefile`, `README.md`, `build`, `ci.go`, `climanifest`, `compression`, `demo`, `docker`, `docs`, `e2e`, `fetch`, `moltcsv`, `scaletesting`, and `scripts`, and removed root fetch wiring from `cmd/root.go`.
- RED/GREEN slice 2: contract then failed on `cmd/escape_password.go` and `cmd/fetch`; deleted those plus `cmd/internal/cmdutil/pprof.go`, and trimmed the root command to verify-only.
- Current RED state: new manifest contract fails because `cockroachdb_molt/molt/go.mod` and `go.sum` still retain fetch-only dependency families (`cloud.google.com/go/storage`, `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go`, `golang.org/x/oauth2`, `google.golang.org/api`).
- Next step: run `go mod tidy` in the pruned vendored module, fix any compile fallout it reveals, and turn the dependency-boundary contract green before auditing `testutils` and running full repo checks.
